rationale: The evidence pattern is consistent with Impacket's wmiexec/smbexec lateral movement tooling. EV-001 and A0 show WmiPrvSE.exe spawning cmd.exe with the characteristic '/Q /c <command> 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<timestamp>' output-redirection pattern used by wmiexec to retrieve command output over SMB ADMIN$, and EV-002 confirms calc.exe was actually executed as a child of that cmd.exe at High integrity under corp\Administrator. EV-004 and EV-005 then show a pivot to the smbexec execution method: services.exe writes a registry ImagePath for a service named 'BTOBTO' containing a bat-file drop-execute-delete chain redirecting output to \\127.0.0.1\C$\__output, and EV-005/EV-006 confirm services.exe spawned cmd.exe as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM to execute that bat file — the hallmark smbexec service-creation technique. The combination of two distinct Impacket execution methods (wmiexec then smbexec), use of ADMIN$ and C$ shares for I/O, randomized bat filename, and escalation from Administrator to SYSTEM integrity constitutes confirmed malicious lateral-movement/remote-execution activity.
